title: Decisive: How to Make Better Choices in Life and Work
author: Chip Heath, Dan Heath
excerpt: The Heath brothers identify four 'villains' of decision-making—narrow framing, confirmation bias, short-term emotion, and overconfidence—and provide a systematic process for making better choices in life and work.
keyIdeas: The four villains of decision-making: narrow framing, confirmation bias, short-term emotion, and overconfidence
The WRAP process: Widen your options, Reality-test your assumptions, Attain distance before deciding, Prepare to be wrong
Multitracking — considering multiple options simultaneously — counteracts narrow framing
Vanishing options test and ooching help reality-test assumptions before committing
keyTakeaways: When facing a decision, ask: 'What else could I do?' to break out of narrow either/or framing
Use a 'pre-mortem' to imagine failure before it happens and identify hidden risks
Set a 'bookend' range for estimates to counteract overconfidence
Build a 'tripwire' — a specific trigger that forces a reconsideration of a decision
whoShouldRead: Managers and executives making consequential strategic decisions
Anyone frustrated by their own indecisiveness or post-decision regret
Teams looking for a shared decision-making process
Readers who enjoyed Switch and Made to Stick by the same authors
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking deep psychological theory rather than practical frameworks
Those who prefer probabilistic/quantitative decision tools
